detailed watercolor illustrated poster map of {argument name=\"city name\" default=\"Beijing\"}, in a handmade Chinese travel-sketchbook style on textured cream paper. Use large black brush-calligraphy for the main title {argument name=\"main title\" default=\"北京\"} on the upper left, plus a small red seal reading {argument name=\"seal text\" default=\"京味儿\"}. Build a simplified bird's-eye city map as the background with pale roads, canals, parks, and blocks. Arrange exactly 17 labeled callout illustrations around and within the map: 10 landmark or area scenes, 5 food illustrations, and 2 modern or lifestyle districts. Place the Forbidden City in the center as the main focal point, with Tiananmen below it. Include the following labeled scenes and dishes in Chinese exactly as written: 八达岭长城, 北京烤鸭, 颐和园, 奥林匹克公园, 老北京胡同, 故宫, 炸酱面, 天坛, 驴打滚, 北京大学, 豆汁儿, 天安门广场, 老北京涮羊肉, 什刹海, 国贸CBD, 王府井大街. Add short handwritten Chinese descriptions beside each. Show the Great Wall over green hills, the Summer Palace by water, the Olympic Park with the Bird's Nest, a hutong courtyard with bicycle and rickshaw, the Temple of Heaven, Peking University gate, the Guomao skyline, Wangfujing archway, and Shichahai lake with a boat. Show food vignettes as plated roast duck, noodles, rolled dessert, a fermented drink set, and a copper hotpot with side ingredients. Keep the painting airy and charming with soft watercolor blooms, loose ink outlines, muted greens and blues, imperial reds and ochres, and a nostalgic cultural-tourism poster feeling. Add a left-side handwritten intro paragraph and a torn note in the bottom right with the closing text {argument name=\"closing note\" default=\"北京，传统与现代交融，历史与生活共存，欢迎你来，发现更多美好！\"}."}
